headline: Assessing Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE) After AI Data Center Power MOU With Super Micro
summary: NANO Nuclear Energy (NNE) has drawn fresh attention after signing a Memorandum of Understanding with Super Micro Computer to explore pairing its microreactors with AI server and data center platforms for on site nuclear power. See our latest analysis for NANO Nuclear Energy. After initially jumping on the Super Micro Computer MOU headlines, NANO Nuclear's 7 day share price return of 14.2% and 30 day return of 32.8% have been tempered by a 1 day share price decline of 8.2%. This has left year...

headline: Nvidia Smuggling Allegations Put Export Controls And Investor Risks In Focus
summary: Nvidia is reportedly linked to a $2.5b smuggling scheme involving Super Micro servers and Nvidia GPUs allegedly diverted to China via Thailand. Major Chinese tech firms are cited as end customers, raising questions about how US export controls on advanced chips are being enforced. The situation could attract regulatory and geopolitical attention to Nvidia’s hardware distribution channels and partners. Nvidia (NasdaqGS:NVDA) sits at the center of the global AI hardware market, and this...

headline: Could Nano Nuclear’s Microreactor Deal Quietly Redefine Supermicro’s (SMCI) Long-Term AI Data Center Edge?
summary: In early May 2026, Nano Nuclear Energy announced a non-binding Memorandum of Understanding with Super Micro Computer to explore integrating microreactor-powered, on-site nuclear energy with Super Micro’s AI server and data center platforms for clean, grid-independent AI infrastructure. This collaboration highlights how power supply is becoming as central as compute in next-generation AI data centers, potentially reshaping how hyperscale and enterprise facilities are designed and...

headline: Consumer Tech News (May 4- 8): Big Tech Gain On AI Momentum, Pentagon Awards $500M AI Contract & More
summary: AI demand surges across chips and cloud as AMD beats estimates, Nvidia expands ecosystem deals, and OpenAI partners scale rapidly across infrastructure and enterprise.
